patch.value:
GitHub: https://github.com/unbraind/pm-cli/issues/469

## Problem
When multiple npm global prefix paths exist, `pm --version` can show a stale version even after `npm install -g @unbrained/pm-cli@latest` — agents conclude the upgrade failed or operate against an old surface.

## Proposed fix
Detect and warn about PATH-shadowed pm binaries (compare active binary path vs installed package), either at --version/doctor time or via a postinstall verification.
GH-469: pm --version reports a stale binary when multiple npm global prefixes exist

Dogfood GH-469: with multiple npm global prefixes (~/.npm-global + homebrew), pm --version can show a stale version even after npm install -g @unbrained/pm-cli@latest. Duplicate check: searched stale version npm global prefixes — no existing item.

pm --version (or pm doctor) detects multiple global installs / PATH-shadowed binaries and warns with the active binary path; or postinstall verifies the active pm on PATH matches the installed package
1. Have two npm global prefixes on PATH (e.g. ~/.npm-global and homebrew). 2. npm install -g @unbrained/pm-cli@latest into one prefix. 3. pm --version resolves the stale binary from the other prefix.
pm --version reflects the just-installed package, or warns that the active binary path does not match the installed package
pm --version silently reports the old version resolved from a different npm global prefix
